summary: Scorching Scarlet>Fire>Estimated suite and fiery Fire on the Mountain
blurb: The second set shines with a blister‑hot Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain → Estimated Prophet sequence and a funk‑filled Not Fade Away. Brent’s keyboards dominate the jams while Jerry delivers scorching solos. Highlights include Cassidy, Alabama Getaway and a high‑energy Good Lovin’.
review: Portland’s June 12, 1980 show is remembered for its volcanic energy – literally, as Mt. St. Helens was erupting nearby – and the band rode that surge straight into one of the most electrifying second‑set suites of the era. The Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain → Estimated Prophet chain erupts with a scorching guitar tone from Jerry, inventive solos on Fire and a soaring, keyboard‑rich Estimated Prophet where Brent Mydland’s harmonies sparkle. Not Fade Away follows the drum intro with a funky groove that keeps the momentum rolling into Black Peter, Around & Around and a rousing Good Lovin’. The first set may be described as solid rather than spectacular, but it still delivers standouts like Alabama Getaway, Cassidy, Althea and Deal, while Bob Weir remains “on” throughout with steady rhythm guitar and vocal work. Audience recordings capture the raw excitement – one even being praised as almost as good as the legendary 11/29/80 show – and a clean soundboard version provides clear vocals and articulate instrument balance. Overall, this performance is a high‑energy snapshot of the Dead in ’80, highlighted by Brent’s keyboard wizardry and Jerry’s fiery lead work.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three of four recordings are clearly positive, repeatedly praising the Scarlet>Fire>Estimated sequence, Brent's keyboard work, and Jerry's scorching solos; one mixed review still notes strong moments. No consistent negative comments appear, so a strong positive adjustment (+1.6) is warranted.

best_recording.reason: Excellent audience tape with clear, balanced mix and low hiss; captures the high‑energy performance and the standout Scarlet>Fire>Estimated sequence almost as well as a top‑tier soundboard.

key_highlights: Scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain → Estimated Prophet suite with Brent’s soaring keyboards.
Fiery Fire On The Mountain performance described as “spitting fire”.
Funky Not Fade Away driven by tight drum intro and groove.
High‑energy Good Lovin’ and encore Johnny B. Goode rounding out the night.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers scorching guitar tone and inventive solos on Fire and Estimated Prophet; vocals clear and articulate, adding fierce energy throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm guitar and vocal work across the set, keeping the band grounded and “on” for the entire show.
band_performance.Brent: Outstanding keyboard performance with great harmonies, heavy involvement in Estimated Prophet and overall texture that shines through the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides a tight drum intro and solid rhythmic foundation, especially evident on the funk‑filled Not Fade Away.
band_performance.Billy: Works seamlessly with Mickey to deliver a powerful percussive backbone throughout both sets.
